meta.title: CMS Operations
meta.excerpt: Strapi CMS operational management and maintenance

seo.metaDescription: Strapi CMS operational management and maintenance

props.text: Complete operational guide for managing Strapi CMS including content management, user administration, role configuration, plugin setup, and data maintenance.

props.body:
URL: https://<strapi-domain>/admin
• Username: Email from initial setup
• Password: Set during first login

1. Visit /admin on new Strapi instance
• 2. Fill out admin user form (email, password)
• 3. Click 'Register'
• 4. Dashboard now accessible

1. Navigate to Content Manager
• 2. Select collection type (Articles, Tutorials, etc)
• 3. Click 'Create new entry'
• 4. Fill in required fields
• 5. Click 'Save' or 'Publish'

Draft - Saved but not published
• Published - Available in API
• Scheduled - Will publish at future date
• Archived - Hidden from API but not deleted

All changes tracked automatically
• View Changes > History to see versions
• Restore: Click 'Restore' on previous version

1. Admin > Users & Permissions > Users
• 2. Click 'Create new user'
• 3. Enter email
• 4. Assign role (Admin, Editor, Contributor, Author)
• 5. Send invitation link to user

Admin - Full access, manage all content and settings
• Editor - Can create, edit, delete all content
• Contributor - Can create and edit own content
• Author - Can only create content (needs editor approval)

Go to Users & Permissions Plugin > Roles
• Select role to customize
• Toggle permissions for each content type
• Can restrict by collection and action

links.title: Database Backups
links.description: For PostgreSQL on Railway.app or similar

props.body:
1. Export via API: GET /api/articles?pagination[pageSize]=1000
• 2. Save JSON response to file
• 3. Keep in version control or secure storage
• 4. Can reimport if needed

1. Contact hosting provider for database restore
• 2. Or manually recreate content from backup
• 3. Verify all content after recovery
• 4. Test API endpoints

Content-Type Builder - Create/edit content types
• Email - Email notifications (SendGrid integration)
• Upload - Media library and file management
• Users & Permissions - User roles and access control
• GraphQL - GraphQL API endpoint

1. Admin > Plugins
• 2. Click plugin to view details
• 3. Enable/Disable toggle
• 4. Restart Strapi if prompted

1. Admin > Media Library
• 2. Drag and drop images/files
• 3. Organize in folders
• 4. Use in content type fields
• 5. Configure upload destination (local or AWS S3)

1. Admin > Content-Type Builder
• 2. Create new Collection Type or Single Type
• 3. Name and description
• 4. Add fields (String, Text, Date, Relation, etc)
• 5. Configure required fields
• 6. Save and deploy

String - Short text (title, email)
• Text - Long text (description, slug)
• Rich Text - Rich HTML editor
• Date - Calendar date picker
• Boolean - Checkbox
• Number - Integer or decimal
• Enumeration - Dropdown choices
• Media - Image/file upload
• Relation - Links to other content
• Component - Reusable field groups
• Dynamic Zone - Multiple block types

Monitor database size and disk usage
• Check for unused media files
• Archive old content periodically
• Review and optimize database indexes
• Update PostgreSQL version when available

Paginate large queries (don't fetch all at once)
• Index frequently filtered fields
• Clean up draft versions regularly
• Monitor slow queries in database logs

Check logs: Strapi Admin > Logs (if available)
• API errors: Monitor API response times
• Database: Check hosting provider dashboard
• Uptime monitoring: Use external service (Uptime Robot)
• Content validation: Test API endpoints regularly
